title: Xenial Dense Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A peppery and dense Algerian dish featuring lamb, capers, and a blend of spices, perfect for a lazy Sunday afternoon. This tagine is marinated in a mixture of olive oil, garlic, and spices, then slow-cooked to perfection.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until fragrant and slightly softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the lamb and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned on all sides, about 10 minutes.
Add the cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper, and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the capers, chicken broth, and browned lamb, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the tagine or Dutch oven and transfer it to the oven, then cook, stirring occasionally, until the lamb is tender, about 2 1/2 hours.
Remove the tagine or Dutch oven from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
